headline: Americas Gold & Silver Hits 4,896 g/t Ag at Galena — Launches Largest-Ever 64,000m Drill Campaign
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver discovered 10 new high-grade veins at its Idaho Galena Complex, including 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company is now launching its largest-ever 64,000m drill program across Galena and Cosalá for 2026.

headline: G Mining Ventures Acquires G2 Goldfields In $2.2B All-Share Deal — JUNIOR GOLD M&A HEATS UP
snippet: G Mining Ventures Corp. agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields Inc. in an all-share transaction valued at approximately $2.2 billion, the latest signal of growing M&A appetite for junior gold producers amid elevated spot prices.

headline: JP Morgan: SHARP PULLBACK In Gold Mining Stocks Has Created 'Compelling Buying Opportunity'
snippet: JP Morgan analysts say the recent correction in gold mining equities has created a compelling entry point. Senior miners have badly lagged the metal — GDX gained ~35-40% while gold surged 60%+ through 2025 — leaving valuations well behind cash flow.

headline: GOLD SLIPS To $4,717 On Profit-Taking — Still Up 47% Year-Over-Year As Bull Run Endures
snippet: Gold fell to $4,717.89/oz on April 13, 2026, down 0.71% on the day but still 46.86% above year-ago levels. The current pullback follows a 5.77% decline over the past month after the metal set a series of record highs.

headline: SILVER Hits $75.60 — Third Straight Weekly Gain On Dollar Weakness And Rate Cut Expectations
snippet: Silver climbed to $75.60/oz on April 10, heading for its third consecutive weekly gain and up over 4% on the week. A weaker US dollar and growing expectations of earlier and deeper Fed rate cuts are fuelling demand for the metal.

headline: COPPER BREAKS $13,000/Tonne On LME — All-Time Record On Supply Squeeze And Tariff Fears
snippet: Copper crossed $13,000/tonne on the LME for the first time, setting an all-time high. The metal has gained 6.6% in 2026 following a 42% surge in 2025, driven by structural supply tightness and tariff-related front-running of inventories.

headline: CHINA Imported 790+ Tonnes Of Silver In Early 2026 — Solar Manufacturing Drives Record Demand
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026, driven largely by surging solar panel production. Silver paste is a critical input for photovoltaic electrical contacts, and the metal has more than tripled in value over the past year.

headline: LITHIUM RESTARTS: Core Lithium To Resume Finniss Mine In May As Spodumene Prices Recover
snippet: Australian producer Core Lithium will restart its Finniss lithium mine in May 2026, targeting first spodumene concentrate delivery by October-December. The restart follows a spodumene price recovery since late 2025 and China's removal of battery export VAT rebates in April.

headline: NexGen Energy To Break Ground At Rook Mine This Summer — Canada's Largest Undeveloped Uranium Deposit
snippet: NexGen Energy will begin construction at its Rook uranium mine in northern Saskatchewan this summer, marking a major milestone for Canada's largest development-stage uranium deposit. The project is a key pillar of domestic supply growth as utility contracting accelerates.

headline: URANIUM RECLAIMS $100/lb — Utility Contracting Demand Re-Emerges After Q1 Geopolitical Sell-Off
snippet: Uranium spot prices staged a strong Q1 rally before geopolitical shocks triggered a sharp sell-off, but prices have recovered back above $100/lb. Analysts note record long-term contracting demand from utilities persists despite short-term spot volatility.

headline: West Red Lake Gold Reports Fresh Assays From Madsen Mine — Red Lake District Results Today
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines released new drill results from its 100%-owned Madsen Mine in the Red Lake Gold District of Northwestern Ontario on April 13, 2026. Red Lake is one of Canada's highest-grade gold districts and a focus of active junior exploration.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m At 0.83 g/t Au At Diablillos — Including 9m At 3.46 g/t Gold In Argentina
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. reported new drill results from its Diablillos project in Argentina, with a highlight intercept of 171m grading 0.83 g/t gold, including a higher-grade zone of 9m at 3.46 g/t Au.

headline: Mining Mega-Deals SURGE 68% — $97B In Billion-Dollar Transactions As Sector Consolidates
snippet: A new industry report reveals mining mega-deals (>$1B) rose 68% compared to 2024, reaching $97B in total value. January 2026 alone saw 85 transactions totalling over $11B, with gold as the most targeted commodity by strategic acquirers.

headline: COT Report April 10: Speculators Trim Gold Longs — What Positioning Data Says About The Next Move
snippet: The April 10 Commitments of Traders report for gold, silver and the US dollar index shows notable shifts in speculative positioning. COT data is closely watched as a contrarian forward indicator for precious metals price direction and mining stock sentiment.

headline: Gold's Next Surge: WHY 2026 COULD BE HISTORIC — Bull Cycle Still In Its Early Innings
snippet: An analyst argues gold is in the early phase of a meaningful bull cycle, with central bank accumulation, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand all still intact. A $6,900/oz cycle peak has been cited under specific macro conditions as the metal already sits near $4,700.
